items.title: Introducing Agent HQ: Any agent, any way you work
items.author: github.blog
items.snippet: GitHub has made a series of major AI agent announcements for GitHub Copilot over the past year. The headline announcement is 'Agent HQ,' unveiled at GitHub Universe, which provides a unified interface across GitHub, VS Code, mobile, and the CLI for directing and managing AI-driven tasks. Third-party coding agents from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Cognition, and xAI are now available directly within GitHub Copilot paid subscriptions. A dedicated 'Squad' feature was also introduced to run coordinate

items.title: Scaling Mixture of Experts: Architecture Search for Billion-Parameter Language Models
items.author: huggingface.co
items.snippet:
Hugging Face's blog in early 2026 has been dominated by the release of Meta's Llama 4 series, featuring two flagship models: Llama 4 Maverick (17B active / ~400B total parameters, 128 experts) and Llama 4 Scout (17B active / ~109B total, 16 experts). Both use a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) auto-regressive architecture with native multimodality via early fusion, supporting text and image inputs out of the box.

Other notable releases include Waypoint-1 from Overworld, a real-time interactive video di

items.title: Open LLM Leaderboard Best Models Collection - Hugging Face
items.author: huggingface.co
items.snippet: In early 2026, the open-source LLM landscape has seen several notable releases. Mistral released Mistral Small 4 in March 2026, a 119B total parameter model (6B active) with 128 experts, a 256K context window, and Apache 2.0 licensing. It consolidates capabilities from three prior Mistral models — Magistral (reasoning), Pixtral (multimodal), and Devstral (agentic coding) — into a single unified model. Cohere also released Cohere Transcribe (2B) under Apache 2.0, topping the Hugging Face Open ASR

items.title: GitHub - zjunlp/DataMind: [ICLR/AAAI 2026] Open-Source LLM-Based Data Analysis Agents
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: The open source AI model landscape in early 2026 has seen a surge in community activity across Hugging Face, GitHub, and Reddit. According to Hugging Face's Spring 2026 state-of-open-source report, the mean size of downloaded open models rose from 827M parameters in 2023 to 20.8B in 2025, driven by quantization and mixture-of-experts (MoE) architectures. A standout release included a 397B MoE model (17B active params) excelling at coding, math, vision, and instruction-following. Three South Kore

items.title: Trendshift — Explore open-source repositories and insights
items.author: trendshift.io
items.snippet: This week on GitHub, the AI agent ecosystem continues to see explosive growth in both new frameworks and community adoption. The standout project is OpenClaw, which has surged past 210,000 stars and is widely cited as one of the fastest-growing open-source projects in GitHub history — covering developer workflow automation, browser automation, web scraping, and proactive scheduling. Alongside it, Dify (130k+ stars) and Open WebUI (124k+ stars) are leading the visual/drag-and-drop wave, while Oll

items.title: New Plugins on the Cursor Marketplace · Cursor
items.author: cursor.com
items.snippet: Cursor has been the most active in releasing updates in early 2026. On March 11, Cursor launched 30+ new marketplace plugins from partners including Atlassian, Datadog, GitLab, Glean, Hugging Face, monday.com, and PlanetScale — most containing MCPs that cloud agents can invoke. On March 19, Cursor released Composer 2 with frontier-level coding performance on challenging tasks. Cursor 2.3 also introduced Automations for always-on agents triggered by Slack, Linear, GitHub, PagerDuty, and webhooks,

items.title: Global AI Law and Policy Tracker: Highlights and Takeaways | IAPP
items.author: iapp.org
items.snippet:
The EU AI Act continues its phased rollout in 2026, with the majority of its provisions becoming applicable on August 2, 2026. This includes obligations for most high-risk AI systems, transparency requirements for AI-generated content (deepfakes, public interest text), and labeling mandates. High-risk AI systems embedded in product safety components face a later deadline of August 2027. The EU framework remains the most comprehensive binding AI law globally.

items.author: arxiv.org
items.snippet: This week on arXiv, several significant papers on AI agents and LLMs emerged. A comprehensive survey titled 'Large Language Model Agent: A Survey on Methodology, Applications and Challenges' (2503.21460) systematically deconstructs LLM agent architectures through a methodology-centered taxonomy covering collaboration mechanisms and evolutionary pathways.
